prompt: Create a vertical cyberpunk manga poster in a gritty two-color screen-print style, using only deep black ink and electric cobalt blue paper texture. Show a dramatic half-body portrait of a young adult anime man with messy, shaggy black hair, turned slightly to the viewer's right, wearing a high-collared futuristic trench coat with angular lapels and technical seams. His face is intentionally hidden by one centered opaque square block, medium blue, covering the face from forehead to chin like a censorship or missing-image placeholder. Behind and around him, compose chaotic graphic design elements: exactly 5 oversized black letter-like typographic forms, including a huge angular “A” in the upper left, a lowercase-style curved form near the top center, a tall diagonal blade-like form in the upper right, a large “e” shape on the right side, and a large tilted “e” shape in the lower left. Add dense technical doodles across the background: arrows, orbit rings, target circles, small diamond icons, circuit-like lines, hatch marks, tiny code blocks, and geometric annotations, all hand-drawn in black. The composition should feel like a distressed zine cover or manga volume cover, with heavy ink splatter, scratches, halftone grain, rough photocopy texture, and a thick worn black border around the entire image. Use a portrait canvas, roughly 3:4 aspect ratio, high contrast, no realistic color shading, no clean modern gradients except the flat blue face-covering square. Keep the typography mostly abstract and unreadable, and do not add any extra readable title text.
